Frontend Island

I entered programming through the frontend.

At first it was visuals, motion and interfaces.

Then came APIs, data, systems and architecture.

I started programming through frontend development. What first attracted me was visuals, interactions and interfaces.

Over time I became curious about how APIs worked, how data moved, how applications communicated.

Looking back, it was difficult for my younger self. Understanding logic, state, APIs and architecture was hard.

But the hardest part was not learning to walk. It was getting up every time I failed.
Action
const response = await fetch('/api/data');
const data = await response.json();
"How does it know?" I wondered. The interface wasn't just drawing pixels anymore. It was conversing with systems.
200 OK
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": [
    { "id": 1, "type": "architecture" },
    { "id": 2, "type": "state" }
  ]
}

The Palette

JavaScript
TypeScript
React
Next.js
Vue
Tailwind CSS
Framer Motion
GSAP
Three.js
HTML5
CSS3
APIs
Responsive Design
UI Engineering
Motion Systems
Chapter 1

Ecosystem Reimagined

A complex web platform visualizing data flow across microservices. What started as simple dashboard views evolved into an interactive nodes-and-edges architecture diagram.

React Three.js GraphQL Tailwind
Chapter 2

Stateful Symphonies

An audio-reactive interface where application state is mapped directly to generative sound and visuals. A deep dive into complex state management and Web Audio APIs.

Vue Pinia Web Audio API Canvas
Chapter 3

Cinematic Router

A custom routing solution that treats page transitions like film cuts and crossfades, abandoning the standard instant-jump for a deliberate, emotional pacing.

TypeScript GSAP Astro Framer Motion

The Interaction Lab

A small playground for frontend motion. Hover, interact, and feel the physics.
